candidates.title: Negative Result: Limits of Consistency-Based Activation Energy for Problem-Level Routing
candidates.summary: Systematic falsification that consistency-derived Ea cannot predict single-pass correctness (AUC=0.436). Includes diagnostic analysis (H4) of why Ea fails, decoupling evidence (H5: Ea vs k_0 unrelated), and explicit boundary map of the Activation Energy framework. Cross-validates ACAR's agreement-but-wrong ceiling with a larger ~25pp quantification.
hypotheses: H2: Ea correlates with difficulty - CONFIRMED (Spearman=0.448)
H3: Ea predicts single-pass solveability - FALSIFIED (AUC=0.436)
H4: Ea measures stability not correctness - PENDING (error classification)
H5: Ea and k_0 are related - FALSIFIED (Spearman=-0.219)
candidates.pilot_focus: H4 error classification on low-Ea failures; P1 pre-registered threshold validation
candidates.key_reference: Yang et al. 2025 (2508.16456) - COLLISION on exponential formula; ACAR 2026 (2602.21231) - cross-validates agreement-but-wong; Li 2026 (2601.00828) - supports error depth framing
novelty_flags: Novel: First systematic falsification of Ea-based routing on MATH benchmark
Novel: ~25pp ceiling quantification for convergence-rate-based routing (vs ACAR's 8pp)
Novel: Ea-k_0 decoupling evidence reveals two unrelated 'difficulty' constructs
Novel: Diagnostic analysis linking routing failure to stability-vs-correctness distinction

ablation_groups.description: Classify low-Ea failures: execution vs conceptual vs extraction errors
Re-run H3 with pre-registered threshold to eliminate data leakage concern

pilot_criteria.H4: Any error mix supports narrative; execution errors >50% strongly supports
pilot_criteria.P1: AUC <0.5 with pre-registered threshold confirms falsification

candidates.status_reason: Front-runner - honest negative result with clear diagnostic path; strongest literature positioning

candidates.title: Expanded Negative Result (n=250)
candidates.summary: Same negative-result framing as cand_a but with expanded sample size (n=250 or full MATH n=500) to address sample size criticism. Expected to replicate all findings with stronger statistical power.
hypotheses: H_B1: All Round 4 findings replicate at n=250
H_B2: Per-problem fit failure rate remains >70%
H_B3: AUC remains <0.5 with pre-registered threshold
candidates.pilot_focus: Re-run G0-G3 on stratified n=250 sample (50 per MATH level)
novelty_flags: Same as cand_a but with stronger statistical power
Novel: Cross-validation with train/test threshold split
candidates.status_reason: Backup - execute if cand_a receives sample size criticism from reviewers

candidates.title: Cross-Model Validation
candidates.summary: Test whether Ea-routing failure generalizes across model families (DeepSeek-Math-7B, Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B, Qwen2.5-Math-7B). Compare saturation parameters and routing AUC across models.
hypotheses: H_C1: Ea-routing failure replicates across model families
H_C2: Aggregate Arrhenius saturation holds across models
H_C3: Ea-difficulty correlation holds across models
candidates.pilot_focus: Run G0-G3 on DeepSeek-Math-7B and Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B
candidates.key_reference: Yang et al. 2025 (cross-model saturation); ACAR 2026 (model-agnostic routing)
novelty_flags: Novel: First cross-model validation of Ea-routing failure
candidates.status_reason: Backup - execute if cand_a receives model-specificity criticism

candidates.title: Error Depth Targeted Training (EDTT)
candidates.summary: If negative result paper is rejected, pivot to training-based approach. Classify errors by depth (L1/L2/L3) and train GRPO with depth-aware rewards. GPU now available via PyTorch 2.11.0.
hypotheses: H_D1: EDTT reduces L3 error rate more than standard GRPO
H_D2: Error depth targeting improves over generic training by >5%
candidates.pilot_focus: Classify errors by depth; train GRPO with depth-aware rewards on small subset
candidates.key_reference: Li 2026 (2601.00828) - Error Depth Hypothesis; S-GRPO (2504.20834); SwS (2506.08989)
novelty_flags: Novel: First error-depth-targeted training for LLM reasoning
Novel: Depth-aware reward shaping in GRPO
candidates.status_reason: Backup - higher complexity; only if cand_a is rejected and GPU training is viable

candidates.title: H1-H2 Validation Paper (Minimalist Fallback)
candidates.summary: If all routing analysis fails, publish H1-H2 validation + H3 negative result as minimalist paper. Acknowledge Yang et al. collision explicitly. Lowest novelty but safest fallback.
hypotheses: H_E1: Arrhenius kinetics generalizes across models
H_E2: Ea-difficulty correlation holds for multiple model families
candidates.status_reason: Fallback - used if cand_a, cand_b, cand_c all fail. Requires acknowledging Yang et al. collision.

pivot_summary.round_5_results: H1+H2 confirmed (aggregate patterns), H3 falsified (AUC=0.436), H5 falsified (Ea vs k_0 unrelated)
pivot_summary.direction_change: Shift from theoretical framework to empirical boundary-delineation and negative-result paper
pivot_summary.novelty_claim: First systematic falsification of Ea-based routing + ~25pp ceiling quantification + diagnostic analysis
pivot_summary.critical_collision: Yang et al. 2025 (2508.16456) - exponential saturation formula; acknowledged, not claimed as novel

execution_plan.immediate: H4 error classification + P1 pre-registered threshold validation
execution_plan.if_H4_supports: Proceed to paper writing with negative-result framing
execution_plan.if_P1_confirms: H3 falsification is robust; proceed with paper
execution_plan.if_P1_reverses: Re-evaluate H3; consider signal comparison (Alt D)
execution_plan.if_paper_rejected: Pivot to cand_c (cross-model) or cand_d (EDTT training)
